blocks.content: The girls arrived together, which Ethan had been told to expect but which still had a quality of arrival that made the room feel different.
Lena came in first, animated, already mid-sentence with something she'd been saying to Cassandra on the way here —

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: — and I'm telling you it had nothing to do with the angle, the problem was the lighting —

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: The lighting was not fine, it was doing something specific that you were just not accounting for —

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I accounted for it, I just chose to —
They both registered the room and stopped mid-conversation, the way people do when they arrive somewhere and have to take it in before they can continue.

She was looking at the candle at the back table. Then she looked at Mr. Alvarez, and something moved briefly across her expression — warmth and recognition — before she crossed the room to him and put her arms around him.
He received it with the same steady warmth he always had, one hand on her back.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Alvarez
blocks.content: The good tea is on the counter.
He said it for her only.
She stepped back. Her eyes were very slightly bright.
Lena had already found Nathan, who had risen from his seat with the instinct of someone who had been well-raised and was also slightly showing off.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: Nathan. We've heard things about you.
